query: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak confirmed case count latest update late June and July 2026. Have any new cases been reported after 21 June 2026 bringing Uganda total above 20? Check ECDC CDTR Week 27, WHO Disease Outbreak News.
answer:
## Summary

**Direct answer: No — Uganda has NOT gone above 20 confirmed cases.** No new cases have been reported in Uganda after 21 June 2026.

**Key findings:**

- **ECDC (page last updated 1 July 2026, reflecting Week 27 CDTR / 29 June update):** Uganda stands at **20 confirmed cases and 2 deaths**, and ECDC explicitly states **no new cases since 21 June 2026**. 15 patients have recovered.

- **WHO Disease Outbreak News:** DON607 and DON608 both list Uganda at **19 confirmed cases, 2 deaths** (WHO figures run one below ECDC/Uganda MoH due to reporting lag). DON608 combined total: 915 cases (896 DRC + 19 Uganda), 234 deaths.

- **Corroboration:** Wikipedia (2 Uganda deaths as of 30 June), NICD (26 June update), and the ebola.fyi tracker all confirm Uganda at 20 cases / 2 deaths with a plateau since 21 June.

- **Context:** The outbreak's growth is driven entirely by the DRC (1,274 cases as of 27 June → 1,333 by 30 June). Uganda's count has been flat at 20 for over a week. Outbreak declared 15 May 2026; PHEIC declared 16 May 2026; caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus (no approved vaccine).

Findings delivered via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: Uganda: 20 confirmed cases, 2 deaths; no new cases since 21 June 2026; page last updated 1 July 2026 17:40; DRC 1,333 confirmed cases as of 30 June 2026.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (DON607)
sources.snippet: In total, 695 confirmed cases; 676 from the DRC and 19 from Uganda; and 138 deaths including two from Uganda.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (DON608)
sources.snippet: In total, 915 confirmed cases; 896 from the DRC and 19 from Uganda; and 234 deaths including two from Uganda.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Two deaths in Uganda as of 30 June; caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus; PHEIC declared 16 May 2026.

sources.title: Situational update on the Ebola disease outbreak caused by Bundibugyo virus (26 June 2026) - NICD
sources.snippet: As of 25 June 2026, a cumulative total of 1,176 confirmed cases; France reported first imported case on 24 June 2026.

sources.title: Uganda: Bundibugyo Ebola Virus Disease Outbreak 2026 - DREF Operational Update (MDRUG058)
sources.snippet: Outbreak declared 15 May 2026; index case 59-year-old male from DRC who sought care in Kampala.

key_facts: Uganda remains at 20 confirmed Bundibugyo Ebola cases and 2 deaths as of the ECDC page last updated 1 July 2026.
ECDC explicitly states no new confirmed cases have been reported in Uganda since 21 June 2026.
No update reviewed brings Uganda's total above 20; the answer to 'have new cases pushed Uganda above 20?' is NO.
Of Uganda's 20 cases, ~15 had DRC travel links and ~5 were local transmission; 15 individuals have recovered.
WHO DON607 listed Uganda at 19 confirmed cases (2 deaths); combined total 695 (676 DRC + 19 Uganda), 138 deaths.
WHO DON608 listed Uganda at 19 confirmed cases (2 deaths); combined total 915 (896 DRC + 19 Uganda), 234 deaths.
WHO DON figures (19) run slightly below ECDC/Uganda MoH figures (20) due to reporting lag/reconciliation; both agree on 2 Uganda deaths.
DRC is driving the outbreak: 1,274 confirmed cases/360 deaths as of 27 June (ECDC 29 June update) and 1,333 confirmed cases by 30 June.
Outbreak declared 15 May 2026; WHO declared PHEIC on 16 May 2026; caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus with no approved vaccine.
France reported its first imported case (returning healthcare worker from DRC) on 24 June 2026.
The ECDC Week 27 CDTR / 29 June 2026 update reaffirmed Uganda at 20 cases, 2 deaths, no new cases since 21 June.
